edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback's existing specs (rp3w7k9d) all use default EMA_200 (trend) and EMA_50 (slope). EMA_200 is a LAGGING trend filter — it takes 200+ bars to react to a regime change. In crypto, regimes shift faster than EMA_200 can track, meaning the pullback signal misses the first 50-100 bars of a new trend (the bars where momentum continuation is strongest). This spec tests FAST-EMA pullback: EMA_100 (trend) and EMA_21 (slope) detect trends 2x faster than the defaults. The tradeoff: faster EMAs produce more false signals in choppy markets. But at extreme R:R (6:1-8:1), false signals are cheap (1.5x ATR stop loss) while true early-trend entries produce large continuation legs (10.5-12.0x ATR TP). The hypothesis is that fast-EMA pullback captures a DIFFERENT set of trades than slow-EMA pullback — specifically, the high-momentum early-trend entries that EMA_200 filters out because the slow EMA hasn't caught up yet. If PF > 1.2 with 80+ trades, fast-EMA pullback is a distinct strategy from slow-EMA pullback and should be traded as a separate portfolio component. If PF < 1.0, the faster EMAs introduce too much noise and EMA_200 is the correct trend filter for RSI pullback. The ultra-fast variant (EMA_50/EMA_9) tests the extreme — can RSI pullback work with near-instant trend detection? Regime assumption: ALL (fast EMAs naturally adapt to regime changes). Invalidation: If all 3 variants produce PF < 1.0, fast-EMA pullback generates too many false signals regardless of R:R.

variants.name: rsi_fast_pullback_1h_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h using fast trend detection: EMA_100 (trend) + EMA_21 (slope) instead of default EMA_200/EMA_50. RSI <= 40 for long entry (standard). The faster EMAs detect uptrend ~100 bars earlier than EMA_200 on 1h — meaning we catch pullbacks in the FIRST DAY of a new trend rather than waiting 8+ days for EMA_200 to confirm. Combined with 7:1 R:R (matching MACD champion), this targets the high-momentum continuation legs that occur in early trends. Expected: 100-200 trades (more than slow-EMA due to faster trend detection), PF 1.1-1.5 if hypothesis holds.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 (fast trend confirmation — price above 100-period average).
EMA_21 slope is positive (current EMA_21 > previous bar EMA_21).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 (fast trend confirmation — price below 100-period average).
EMA_21 slope is negative (current EMA_21 < previous bar EMA_21).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Fast trend filter: EMA_100 (not EMA_200) for earlier trend detection
Fast slope: EMA_21 (not EMA_50) for quicker slope confirmation
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 7:1. Needs 12.5% win rate to break even after fees.
Early-trend capture: fast EMAs detect trends ~100 bars before EMA_200
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 fast trend filter — detects trends ~100 bars earlier than EMA_200
RSI_14 <= 40 long pullback — standard RSI threshold, unchanged from default
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 10.5x TP — 7:1 R:R, STOP_FLOOR compliant

variants.name: rsi_fast_pullback_4h_8to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h using fast trend detection: EMA_100 (trend) + EMA_21 (slope). RSI <= 42 for long entry (slightly relaxed to increase trade count on 4h). On 4h, EMA_100 represents ~17 days vs EMA_200's ~33 days. The 17-day trend window better matches crypto's typical trend duration (2-4 weeks for major moves). RSI at 42 (vs 40) adds ~10% more entries while keeping the 'pullback' signal meaningful. Combined with 8:1 R:R, this tests whether the fast-EMA pullback concept scales to 4h where each bar represents more price action. Expected: 40-80 trades (4h reduces frequency), PF target > 1.2.
entry_long: EMA_21 slope is positive (current EMA_21 > previous bar EMA_21).
RSI_14 <= 42 (slightly relaxed pullback threshold for 4h trade count).
entry_short: EMA_21 slope is negative (current EMA_21 < previous bar EMA_21).
RSI_14 >= 58 (slightly relaxed pullback threshold for 4h trade count).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Fast trend filter: EMA_100 for 4h (~17 day trend window)
Fast slope: EMA_21 for quicker slope detection
RSI pullback: RSI_14 <= 42 (relaxed for 4h trade count)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
4h fast-EMA test: does EMA_100 match crypto's 2-4 week trend cycle better than EMA_200?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 on 4h — 17-day trend window, matches crypto's typical 2-4 week trend duration
RSI_14 <= 42 long — slightly relaxed to improve 4h trade count
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP — 8:1 R:R, STOP_FLOOR compliant

variants.name: rsi_ultrafast_pullback_1h_6to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with ULTRA-FAST trend detection: EMA_50 (trend) + EMA_9 (slope). This is the extreme of the fast-EMA thesis. EMA_50 detects trends in ~2 days on 1h (vs ~8 days for EMA_200). EMA_9 slope reacts within hours. The ultra-fast setup will generate the MOST signals (150-300+ trades) but many will be false — noise from choppy sideways action. At 6:1 R:R (the R:R floor), false signals cost 1.5x ATR each, while true trend pullbacks produce 9.0x ATR wins. The math: if true signal rate is >14.3%, this is profitable. RSI <= 38 (tighter than default) adds selectivity to compensate for the faster, noisier EMAs. This variant is the stress test — if it works, speed of trend detection matters more than filtering accuracy. If it fails, there's a speed limit for EMA trend filters.
entry_long: Close > EMA_50 (ultra-fast trend confirmation — 2-day trend window on 1h).
EMA_9 slope is positive (current EMA_9 > previous bar EMA_9 — hourly slope).
RSI_14 <= 38 (tight pullback filter to compensate for fast EMA noise).
entry_short: EMA_9 slope is negative (current EMA_9 < previous bar EMA_9).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Ultra-fast trend: EMA_50 trend + EMA_9 slope — near-instant regime detection
Tight RSI: RSI_14 <= 38 to compensate for noisy fast EMAs
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 6:1. Needs 14.3% win rate to break even after fees.
Ultra-fast stress test: is there a speed limit for EMA trend filters?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 ultra-fast trend — 2-day window on 1h, near-instant regime detection
RSI_14 <= 38 tight pullback — compensates for noisy fast EMA signals
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 9.0x TP — 6:1 R:R floor, STOP_FLOOR compliant
